tier_rationale: Multiple harness operation documents and scaffolding files may be updated; no product runtime behavior changes.
request_summary: Implement a clear Analyst-to-Generator invocation contract using isolated Claude CLI execution.
goal: Define and scaffold the Generator handoff flow so Analyst can invoke Claude CLI without sharing overlapping Analyst/Researcher context.

positive: Generator must be invoked as a fresh Claude CLI execution with task-scoped input.
Analyst must pass only minimal, selected context.
Researcher outputs must pass through Analyst and include only summary, snippets, confidence, and unverified claims.
The design must record command shape and artifact paths without secrets.
negative: Do not pass the full Analyst conversation to Generator.
Do not pass full Researcher source documents or unbounded URLs to Generator.
Do not pass Validator private context or other Validator results to Generator.
Do not include API keys, environment variable values, passwords, PII, or secrets.
Do not modify product runtime behavior in this task.

success_criteria: The Analyst-to-Generator handoff contract is documented with allowed and forbidden context.
The Claude CLI invocation model is documented as fresh, task-scoped execution.
The expected Generator input and output artifacts are specified.
Ledger events for Generator instruction and result capture are specified.
Validation instructions for the next Validator handoff remain compatible with Tier 2 and Tier 3 flows.
Repository audits pass for the changed harness files.

context.relevant_history: TASK-20260427-091 established that Generator integration should begin with an isolated handoff contract rather than a shared conversation or full runtime automation.

artifact_refs.description: Planning decision for isolated Claude CLI Generator invocation.
